meta.description: Vulnerability Database Index - maps vulnerability types to their corresponding files
meta.usage: Agents should use this index to quickly find relevant vulnerability patterns based on keywords, categories, or protocol context

oracle.description: Oracle integration vulnerabilities including price feeds, randomness, and cross-chain messaging

price_manipulation.description: Flash loan and spot price oracle manipulation attacks

concentrated-liquidity.description: Uniswap V3 style concentrated liquidity AMM issues

general.description: Common vulnerability patterns applicable across protocols

bridge.description: Cross-chain bridge vulnerability patterns from real exploits

flash-loan.description: Flash loan attack patterns and mechanism exploits

validation.description: Input validation and slippage protection vulnerabilities from real DeFi exploits

token-compatibility.description: Non-standard token behavior vulnerabilities including reflection, rebasing, ERC777, and self-transfer bugs

missing-validation.description: Input validation and access control issues

signature-verification.description: Signature verification vulnerabilities including replay attacks, malleability, and ecrecover issues

storage-collision.description: Storage collision vulnerabilities in proxy contracts and upgradeable systems

initialization.description: Initialization vulnerabilities in upgradeable and proxy contracts

mev-bot.description: MEV bot vulnerabilities and exploitation patterns

access-control.description: Access control and authorization vulnerabilities from real DeFi exploits

proxy-patterns.description: Comprehensive proxy pattern vulnerabilities covering all proxy types

files.name: PROXY_PATTERN_VULNERABILITIES.md
focus: permissions allowances not reset on transfer

business-logic.description: Business logic flaw vulnerabilities covering state transitions, calculation errors, and protocol-specific logic issues

files.name: defi-business-logic-flaws.md
focus: improper token handling burn mint transfer

arbitrary-call.description: Arbitrary external call vulnerabilities in routers, aggregators, and bridges

cosmos.description: Cosmos SDK and app-chain specific vulnerabilities

gas-metering.description: Gas metering and EVM compatibility issues

unique.description: Protocol-specific unique vulnerability patterns from real exploits

amm-exploits.description: Unique AMM vulnerability patterns from real-world exploits

searchIndex.description: Quick lookup by common search terms - maps keywords to relevant files

protocolContext.description: Recommended files based on protocol type being audited - start with these for each protocol type

staking_rewards.description: Staking platforms, reward distribution, dividend tokens

token_launch.description: New token launches, meme coins, token contracts with trading functionality

auditChecklist.description: Quick checklist items organized by vulnerability type
general: Check all external calls for reentrancy
Verify slippage protection on all swaps
Check oracle staleness and manipulation vectors
Verify access control on sensitive functions
Check for first depositor/inflation attacks on vaults
Verify rounding direction favors the protocol
staking_rewards: Check reward calculation uses internal tracking vs external balanceOf
Verify proper time-based checkpointing for rewards
Check rewards cannot be claimed multiple times for same period
Verify deposit amount validated against actual transfer received
Check donation cannot immediately affect dividend per share
Verify buyFor/depositFor cannot be called with contract as recipient
Check for integer overflow/underflow in calculations
Verify k-value validated AFTER swap callbacks complete
Check transfer(self, 0) doesn't trigger unintended effects
Verify skim() cannot be abused after balance manipulation
proxy: Check constructor calls _disableInitializers()
Verify initialize() has initializer modifier
Check atomic initialization with proxy deployment
Verify all base contracts have storage gaps (__gap)
Confirm using @openzeppelin/contracts-upgradeable libraries
Check EIP-1967 compliant storage slots
Verify _authorizeUpgrade has proper access control (UUPS)
Check implementation cannot be called directly
Verify no selfdestruct or delegatecall to user-controlled addresses
Check clone validation verifies full bytecode
Verify implementation is not metamorphic
Check storage layout unchanged between versions
Verify no msg.value reuse in batch delegatecalls
Check module upgrades include state migration
Verify permissions/plugins cleared on proxy ownership transfer
Check token allowances handled on proxy ownership transfer
Verify owner cannot be changed during delegatecall execution
Check CREATE2 salt includes owner address (not just tx.origin)
Verify counterfactual wallet address depends on entrypoint
Check beacon implementation has code (code.length > 0)
Verify no approvals/state set in constructor (use initialize)
Check no storage variables initialized at declaration
Verify whitelists account for upgradeable proxy risk
Check registry handles selfdestructed proxies
